content: For a tokenized portfolio with many whitelisted investor wallets, ERC-3643 is the strongest Product Setup architecture target because permissioned holding and transfer checks are central. ERC-721 can be explained if asked, but it is out of MVP scope for ZiLi-OS because this workflow focuses on fungible investment units, vault shares, permissioned fungible tokens, and rebasing balances.

suggestedRequirementUpdates.rationale: The MVP describes whitelisted investor wallets and restricted token holding.

suggestedRequirementUpdates.proposedValue: up to 50 whitelisted investor wallet addresses
suggestedRequirementUpdates.rationale: The current MVP scale is one asset manager and up to 50 investor wallets.

openQuestions: Do only approved wallets need to hold or receive the token?
What valuation file fields will be uploaded daily?

protocolComparison.erc20: Simple fungible investment units where restrictions can stay mostly in ZiLi-OS workflows or custom extensions.
protocolComparison.erc4626: Best for a clean single-asset vault where investors deposit one ERC-20 asset and receive vault shares.
protocolComparison.erc3643: Best fit when approved wallets, permissioned holding, and transfer checks are central to the product.
protocolComparison.rebasingErc20: Use only when investor balances should automatically adjust after NAV, value, or yield updates.
protocolComparison.erc721OutOfScope: Useful for unique non-fungible token IDs, but not an active ZiLi-OS MVP protocol base.
protocolComparison.recommendation: Recommend ERC-3643 when whitelisted wallets and restricted transfers are core requirements.

riskNotes: This is product-engineering guidance, not legal, investment, or formal audit advice.
Generated Solidity remains demo/testnet scaffold until compiled, tested, QA-reviewed, and security-reviewed.
nextRecommendedAction: Confirm the recommended protocol base before Contract Ops prepares deployment settings.
